title: 11 Herbal Tinctures For Ringworm
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a good option for treating ringworm, a fungal infection that causes a red, itchy rash. Herbal teas can help relieve ringworm because they contain compounds that have antifungal properties, which can help fight the infection. One herb that's been used for this purpose is Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort. It contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects. This means it can help reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms and speed up the healing process.  Another herb that's been used to treat ringworm is Eucalyptus globulus, or blue gum eucalyptus. It contains a compound called eucalyptol, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties. This can help prevent the infection from spreading and reduce the risk of complications.  Teucrium chamaedrys, or wood sage, is also a herb that's been used to treat ringworm. It contains compounds that have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of symptoms and speed up the healing process.  Using herbal teas to treat ringworm can be beneficial because they're a natural and non-invasive way to treat the infection. They can also be a good option for people who are allergic to conventional medications or prefer a more natural approach to healthcare. Additionally, herbal teas can be made from a variety of herbs, which can be combined to create a custom formula that's tailored to the individual's needs. This can be a good option for people who have sensitive skin or are prone to allergic reactions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures contains hyperforin and hypericin, two biologically active constituents that have antifungal properties. These compounds can inhibit the growth of fungi, including the ones that cause ringworm. The antifungal properties of hyperforin and hypericin are due to their ability to disrupt the fungal cell membrane and inhibit the production of fungal toxins. Additionally, hyperforin has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help the body's natural defenses fight off the infection. By using Hypericum perforatum tinctures, you may be able to reduce the severity and duration of ringworm symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves. Dry them completely in a warm, dark place.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers and leaves with 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops of the tincture in a cup of water 3-4 times a day for ringworm treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures contains several bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and beta-pinene. These compounds have antifungal properties, which help combat fungal infections like ringworm. Eucalyptol, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the severity of ringworm symptoms. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptus constituents help soothe and protect the affected skin, promoting a faster recovery. By applying eucalyptus globulus tinctures to the affected area, the antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects can help manage ringworm and support the healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves to the jar and cover them with vodka. Make sure the leaves are completely covered.
Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label the jar with the date and contents, and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the tincture, 2-3 times a day, on the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Teucrium chamaedrys tinctures contains the bioactive constituents labiatic acid, teucrin, and teucrin A. These compounds have antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like ringworm. The antifungal activity of labiatic acid and teucrin can inhibit the growth of the fungus Trichophyton, the primary cause of ringworm. Teucrin A also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and itching associated with ringworm. By targeting the root cause of the infection and reducing symptoms, Teucrium chamaedrys tinctures can be an effective treatment for ringworm.
remedies.image_alt: Teucrium chamaedrys herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of fresh or dried Teucrium chamaedrys leaves.
Combine the leaves with 750ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Label the jar with the date and contents, and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops of the tincture in water 3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures contains boiactive constituents such as cineole, linalool, and terpinen-4-ol, which have antifungal properties that help combat ringworm. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause ringworm. Terpinen-4-ol has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms, such as redness and itching. Linalool also contributes to the antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects of Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures, making it an effective treatment for ringworm. The antiseptic and antifungal properties of these constituents work together to reduce the fungal infection that causes ringworm.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, 2 cups of 80-proof vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Chop the leaves and add them to the jar.
Pour the vodka over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains bioactive constituents like alkylamides, phenolic acids, and volatile oils. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm. The tannins present in Echinacea purpurea also have antifungal properties, making it difficult for the fungus that causes ringworm to grow and multiply. The alkaloids, specifically echinacoside and caftaric acid, have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal effects, further contributing to its potential benefits for ringworm. By targeting the underlying causes of ringworm, Echinacea purpurea tinctures may provide relief from symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Combine the Echinacea flowers and leaves with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Bottle the tincture and store it in the fridge. Use 10-20 drops, 3-4 times a day, on the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help combat ringworm, a skin infection caused by fungal pathogens. The flavonoids in Calendula officinalis tinctures, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells and reduce inflammation. The triterpenoids, including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, have antimicrobial properties that help fight off fungal infections. By applying Calendula officinalis tinctures to affected areas, it can help to reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers, 2 cups of 80-proof vodka, and a glass jar with a lid.
Combine the Calendula flowers and vodka in the glass jar, making sure the flowers are covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the Calendula tincture to a dropper bottle and use 5-10 drops, 2-3 times a day, directly on the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures contains compounds like quinine and alkaloids, which have antifungal properties. These compounds help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, a common skin infection. Quinine, in particular, has been shown to have antifungal activity against Trichophyton species, a common cause of ringworm. The tannins and flavonoids present in Cinchona officinalis tinctures also contribute to its antifungal properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin. By using Cinchona officinalis tinctures topically, the antifungal properties can help to reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis bark and chop it into small pieces.
Combine 1 cup of chopped Cinchona officinalis bark with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and shake it well for 2-3 minutes to mix the ingredients.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day to help the tincture extract.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Use 10-15 drops of the tincture, 3 times a day, on the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tinctures contains a high concentration of silymarin, a potent flavonoid complex. Silymarin is known for its powerful antifungal properties, which are effective against fungal infections like ringworm. The tannins present in the tincture have astringent properties, helping to dry out the affected area and reduce fungal growth. Silybum marianum also contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to inhibit fungal enzymes and disrupt fungal cell membranes. By using Silybum marianum tinctures, you can harness the antifungal and antiseptic properties of silymarin and tannins to help combat ringworm.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Combine the leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar. Add 2 cups of 80 proof vodka. Stir well.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label the jar with the date and the name of the tincture. Store it in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture, 3 times a day, as needed, to treat ringworm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains aloin and aloe-emodin, two potent compounds that help combat ringworm. Aloin, a yellow pigment, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that kill the fungus causing ringworm. Aloe-emodin, a lignan, inhibits fungal growth and reduces inflammation, making it an effective treatment for ringworm. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe-emodin also help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. By targeting the fungus and reducing inflammation, aloe barbadensis tinctures can help clear up ringworm infections.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Aloe barbadensis leaves and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Chop the Aloe leaves into small pieces and add them to the jar with vodka. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After the sitting period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the Aloe tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops of the tincture, mixed with water, 3-4 times a day to treat ringworm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium tinctures contains the bioactive constituents thujone, borneol, and camphor. These compounds have antifungal properties that help combat the fungus causing ringworm. Thujone has been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungi, including the Trichophyton species responsible for ringworm. Borneol and camphor have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and protect the affected skin. By using Artemisia absinthium tinctures, the antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate ringworm symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers.
Combine the leaves and flowers with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 10-20 drops of the tincture in water or a carrier oil as needed to treat ringworm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tinctures contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which have been found to have antifungal properties. These compounds help to inhibit the growth of fungal spores, including those that cause ringworm. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically taraxasterol and inulin, have been shown to have a direct impact on fungal cells, disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from reproducing. The flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in affected skin. By targeting the fungal infection at the source and reducing inflammation, Taraxacum officinale tinctures can help to alleviate the symptoms of ringworm.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal tinctures for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale roots, flowers, and leaves. Clean and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped Taraxacum officinale with 2 cups of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar. The jar should be filled to the top.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture, 3-4 times a day, for ringworm treatment.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent ringworm. Some herbal teas, like tea tree oil and peppermint, have antifungal properties that can stop the fungus that causes ringworm from growing. However, it's not a proven method, and ringworm can still develop even with herbal tea consumption.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to treating ringworm, but consuming them daily may not be the best idea. Some herbs can be too strong and might cause skin irritation or interact with other medications. It's best to limit their use to when symptoms are worst and then gradually stop or reduce the amount as the ringworm heals.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with ringworm, but it may take some time to see results. Some people notice improvement in a few days to a week, while others may take a week or two to notice a change. It's different for everyone and depends on the severity of the infection.
faq_time: To drink herbal tea for ringworm, try having it in the morning. This helps your body start the day with a boost of healing properties from the tea. The morning also gives you the rest of the day to let the tea work its way through your system and help clear up the infection.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with some prescription medications, but it's not a guarantee. Some herbal teas may slow down or speed up the way your body processes certain medications, including those for ringworm. This could affect how well the medications work or increase the risk of side effects.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be careful with herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like tea tree oil, can be helpful for ringworm, but others might not be safe for you or your baby. If you're using herbal teas for ringworm, stick to ones that have been tested and are known to be safe during pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and dealing with ringworm, herbal teas might seem like a soothing solution. However, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas can pass their properties into breast milk, which may not be suitable for your baby. Always check the safety of the tea and its ingredients before consumption.
title_2: 11 Herbal Tinctures for Athletes to Fight Ringworm
